subject: Outlook keeps crashing when I open large emails
description:
Hi IT support,

I need help with Outlook. It crashes consistently when I try to open certain emails, particularly ones with large inline content. I've tried everything I can think of — cleared the cache, repaired the Office installation, even reinstalled it completely. Nothing helps.

Let me give you some background. I'm in the Wealth Management team and we receive a lot of client correspondence with embedded charts, tables, and sometimes inline images. The problem started about a week ago after the March Windows update. Before that, everything was fine.

Here's what happens step by step:
1. I open Outlook normally — it loads fine
2. I navigate to my inbox — works fine
3. I click on an email from our client reporting system — the preview pane shows the email header but then Outlook freezes
4. After about 10-15 seconds, I get the 'Not Responding' message
5. Then Outlook crashes with an error dialog that says 'Microsoft Outlook has stopped working'
6. Sometimes it offers to restart in safe mode, which I've tried, but the same emails still crash it

I've noticed the problematic emails all seem to be the automated reports from our portfolio management system. These emails typically contain:
- 15-20 inline charts as embedded images
- Large HTML tables with 500+ rows of position data
- Multiple embedded Excel snippets
- The total email size is usually 8-12 MB

I tried forwarding one of these emails to my personal account and opening it in the web browser version of Outlook — it works there, just very slowly. So the data in the email seems fine, it's the desktop client that can't handle it.

Other things I've tried:
- Running Outlook in safe mode (still crashes on these specific emails)
- Creating a new Outlook profile (same issue)
- Disabling all COM add-ins (still crashes)
- Running the Office repair tool (both quick and online repair)
- Checking for Office updates (I'm on the latest version: Microsoft 365 Apps, Version 2403, Build 17425.20228)
- Increasing the Outlook cache size in the registry
- Clearing the Outlook temp folders
- Running SFC /scannow (found no issues)
- Checking Event Viewer — I see application error events for OUTLOOK.EXE with exception code 0xc0000005 (access violation)

My system specs:
- Dell Latitude 7440, 16GB RAM, Intel i7-1365U
- Windows 11 Enterprise 23H2
- 256GB SSD with 89GB free
- Connected via Ethernet in the NYC office, Floor 3

This is really impacting my work because these automated reports are how I track client portfolio performance. I'm currently having to use the web version of Outlook which is much slower and doesn't integrate with our other desktop tools.

I've attached the Event Viewer export showing the crash events. Please let me know if you need any other information.

Thanks for your help,
Melissa

P.S. — My colleague Janet mentioned she had a similar issue last month but hers resolved after an update. Not sure if that helps.

P.P.S. — I also noticed that my Outlook search hasn't been working properly since the crashes started. It shows results from months ago but not recent emails. Might be related to the indexing service getting corrupted by the crashes?

---
Melissa Chang
Senior Portfolio Manager | Wealth Management
Contoso Financial Services
One Financial Plaza, Floor 3
New York, NY 10004
Tel: +1 (212) 555-0147
Email: melissa.chang@contoso.com

CONFIDENTIALITY NOTICE: This email and any attachments are for the exclusive and confidential use of the intended recipient. If you are not the intended recipient, please do not read, distribute, or take action based on this message. If you have received this in error, please notify the sender immediately by return email and delete this message from your system. Contoso Financial Services does not accept liability for any damage caused by any virus transmitted by this email.

subject: FW: RE: RE: RE: RE: Network issues in London office
description:
Adding IT support to this thread. We've been going back and forth for a week now and the issue still isn't resolved.

Current issue: Intermittent packet loss (15-30%) on the London office LAN affecting all users on Floor 2. Started March 11. Network team replaced the switch but it didn't help.

subject: Can't connect to production database - sharing config for reference
description:
Hi team,

I'm unable to connect to the production analytics database from my local development environment. I've been trying different connection strings and nothing works. Here's my current configuration file for reference:

```
[database]
host = prod-analytics-db.contoso.internal
port = 5432
dbname = client_analytics
username = svc_analytics_ro
password = Pr0d-An@lyt!cs_2026#SecurePass
ssl_mode = require
ssl_cert = /etc/ssl/contoso/prod-ca.pem

[azure]
keyvault_url = https://kv-prod-analytics.vault.azure.net/
storage_connection_string = DefaultEndpointsProtocol=https;AccountName=contosoproddata;AccountKey=rK8xT2vN5qW3mJ7pL9bF4hD6gA0cE1iU8oY=;EndpointSuffix=core.windows.net
tenant_id = 72f988bf-86f1-41af-91ab-2d7cd011db47
client_id = a8f3c2e1-4b5d-4f6a-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
client_secret = ~Kv8Q~3xR7mN2pL5wT9vB4hJ6fA0dE1cG8iU
```

The error I get is: 'FATAL: password authentication failed for user "svc_analytics_ro"'

I suspect the service account password was rotated recently and nobody updated the documentation. Can someone verify the current credentials or grant me access through a different method?

Also, I noticed the Azure Key Vault has a new access policy that might be blocking my app registration. Can you check?

Thanks,
Raj

subject: 🚨 Ùrgënt — Ṡÿstem ïssüe wîth spëcial çharacters in døcument namés 📄❌
description:
Hëllo IT Sùpport 👋

I'm having a prøblem with SharePoint. When I try to upload dôcuments with special characters in the filename (like café-report-2026.xlsx or naïve-bayes-modël.py or Ñoño-client-données.pdf), the upload fails with a generic error.

The error message shows: «Erreur: Le nom du fichier contient des caractères non valides» which seems to be appearing in French even though my system is set to English 🤔

具体的に言うと、日本語のファイル名も問題があります。例えば「月次報告書.docx」というファイルもアップロードできません。

I've also noticed that the search index seems corrupted — searching for "naïve" returns zero results even though the file exists (uploaded before this issue started).

Steps to reproduce:
1. Go to https://contoso.sharepoint.com/sites/wealth-mgmt
2. Click Upload → Files
3. Select any file with accented characters (é, ü, ø, ñ, etc.) or CJK characters
4. Upload fails with error

This affects our entire team since we work with international clients and many of our documents have non-ASCII filenames.

Merci beaucoup! 🙏
— Léa Müller

P.S. The issue also affects files with emoji in the name like "📊 Q1 Report.xlsx" but those I can rename. The accented characters are in client names so I can't change those.

subject: EXTREMELY URGENT CRITICAL PRIORITY ONE EMERGENCY HELP NEEDED IMMEDIATELY — VPN connection failure preventing access to all internal systems including email, SharePoint, Teams, and all line-of-business applications after the mandatory security update that was pushed overnight to all laptops in the Singapore office which seems to have broken the GlobalProtect VPN client configuration and now approximately 47 users in the Singapore office cannot work remotely and we have a critical regulatory filing deadline with MAS tomorrow morning at 9 AM SGT and if we miss it there will be significant financial penalties and reputational damage to the firm so this needs to be fixed RIGHT NOW PLEASE
description: Same as subject. VPN is broken for the whole Singapore office after last night's update. GlobalProtect shows 'Gateway not found' error. 47 users affected. MAS filing deadline tomorrow 9 AM.

subject: Data migration errors — partially corrupted export
description:
Running the data migration from the legacy CRM to Dynamics 365. The export file has corruption issues. Here's a sample of what the data looks like:

Record 1: {"client_id": "C-4892", "name": "Meridian Partners LLC", "aum": 45000000}
Record 2: {"client_id": "C-4893", "name": "Ωmega Cα���pital\x00\x00", "aum": NaN}
Record 3: {"cli�ent_id": "C-4894", "n??ame": "Br\xc3\xb8kën Däta Grøup", "aum": -1}
Record 4: \xff\xfe{\x00"\x00c\x00l\x00i\x00e\x00n\x00t\x00_\x00i\x00d\x00 — appears to be UTF-16 encoded
Record 5: {"client_id": "C-4896", "name": "Vanguard Holdings", "aum": 78000000} ← this one is fine
Records 6-847: Similar mix of valid and corrupted entries

About 30% of the 12,000 records have some form of corruption — null bytes, encoding mismatches, NaN values, or truncated fields. The legacy system was running on SQL Server 2008 with mixed collation settings.

We need to:
1. Fix the export process to handle encoding properly
2. Recover the corrupted records from the source database
3. Complete the migration before the legacy system is decommissioned on March 31

The migration team has been working on this for two weeks and we're running out of time.

subject: API gateway returning 502 errors
description:
The API gateway is throwing 502 errors. Here's the last 500 lines of the gateway log:

2026-03-18T06:00:01.234Z [INFO] Gateway started on port 443
2026-03-18T06:00:01.235Z [INFO] Loading route configuration from /etc/apigw/routes.yaml
2026-03-18T06:00:01.240Z [INFO] Route /api/v1/auth -> upstream auth-service:8080 (healthy)
2026-03-18T06:00:01.241Z [INFO] Route /api/v1/clients -> upstream client-service:8080 (healthy)
2026-03-18T06:00:01.242Z [INFO] Route /api/v1/trades -> upstream trade-service:8080 (healthy)
2026-03-18T06:00:01.243Z [INFO] Route /api/v1/reports -> upstream report-service:8080 (healthy)
2026-03-18T06:00:02.100Z [INFO] Health check: auth-service OK (12ms)
2026-03-18T06:00:02.105Z [INFO] Health check: client-service OK (8ms)
2026-03-18T06:00:02.110Z [INFO] Health check: trade-service OK (15ms)
2026-03-18T06:00:02.115Z [INFO] Health check: report-service OK (9ms)
2026-03-18T06:00:05.000Z [INFO] Health check cycle complete. All upstreams healthy.
2026-03-18T06:00:10.000Z [INFO] Health check: auth-service OK (11ms)
2026-03-18T06:00:10.005Z [INFO] Health check: client-service OK (7ms)
2026-03-18T06:00:10.010Z [INFO] Health check: trade-service OK (14ms)
2026-03-18T06:00:10.015Z [INFO] Health check: report-service OK (10ms)
[... 400+ similar health check lines omitted for brevity ...]
2026-03-18T08:47:22.891Z [ERROR] upstream report-service:8080 returned 502: connection refused
2026-03-18T08:47:22.892Z [ERROR] circuit breaker OPEN for report-service after 5 consecutive failures
2026-03-18T08:47:23.100Z [WARN] request POST /api/v1/reports/generate -> 502 Bad Gateway (report-service circuit open)
2026-03-18T08:47:24.200Z [WARN] request GET /api/v1/reports/quarterly -> 502 Bad Gateway (report-service circuit open)
2026-03-18T08:47:25.300Z [ERROR] upstream report-service health check FAILED: connection refused
2026-03-18T08:47:30.000Z [INFO] Health check: auth-service OK (12ms)
2026-03-18T08:47:30.005Z [INFO] Health check: client-service OK (8ms)
2026-03-18T08:47:30.010Z [INFO] Health check: trade-service OK (13ms)
2026-03-18T08:47:30.015Z [ERROR] Health check: report-service FAILED: connection refused
2026-03-18T08:47:35.015Z [ERROR] Health check: report-service FAILED: connection refused
2026-03-18T08:47:40.015Z [ERROR] Health check: report-service FAILED: connection refused

The report-service container crashed. All other services are fine. Checked the report-service logs and it looks like an OOM kill — the pod was using 3.8GB of a 4GB limit when generating a large quarterly report. Kubernetes event shows: 'OOMKilled: Container report-service exceeded memory limit'.

This is affecting all report generation for the platform. Trading and auth services are unaffected.
